data.title: Planet Midday News
data.description:
1. Tether CEO responds to S&P rating: "We take pride in the hatred of rating agencies; the traditional financial system is riddled with holes."
2. Alt5 Sigma, linked to the Trump family's crypto project, fires its CEO and COO.
3. UPBIT: Its SOLANA network suffered a 54 billion won hack; it will compensate all customer losses.
4. Opinion: Bitcoin's fragile rebound is built on shrinking liquidity; large holders are selling.
5. "I'm bullish, you know what I mean."
6. Naver and Upbit's parent company Dunamu will jointly invest $6.8 billion in AI and blockchain.
7. Edel Finance-related wallets "snatched up" 30% of the token supply; co-founder denies allegations.
8. Uniswap now supports the Monad mainnet.
9. An ancient whale that previously interacted with the Ethereum Foundation bought 7,318 ETH at an average price of $3,016, worth $22.07 million.
10. Tether confirms termination of operations in Uruguay and will fire 30... Employees.

data.title: A newly created wallet deposited $3 million USDC into HyperLiquid and placed a short order for HYPE.
data.description: According to Onchain Lens monitoring, a newly created wallet deposited $3 million USDC into HyperLiquid and placed an order to short HYPE in the $35.7-$36.7 range.

data.title: Ripple USD Recognized as Fiat-Anchored Token in Abu Dhabi
data.description: According to PANews, Ripple has announced that its USD-backed stablecoin, Ripple USD (RLUSD), has been recognized by the Abu Dhabi Financial Services Regulatory Authority (FSRA) as a 'recognized fiat-anchored token.' This designation allows RLUSD to be used within the Abu Dhabi Global Market (ADGM), the international financial center in the capital of the United Arab Emirates (UAE).

data.title: Australia Proposes Digital Asset Framework to Enhance Customer Protection
data.description:
According to ChainCatcher, Australia's Treasurer Jim Chalmers and Minister for Financial Services Daniel Mulino presented the 2025 Corporate Amendment (Digital Assets Framework) Bill to Parliament on Wednesday. The legislation aims to address regulatory gaps and enhance the protection of billions in customer assets.

The bill seeks to bring cryptocurrency exchanges and custodial service providers under the regulatory scope of financial services laws, designating the Australian Securities and Investments Commission (ASIC) as the primary regulatory body. The new framework introduces two new categories of financial products: digital asset platforms and tokenized custodial platforms, requiring operators to hold an 'Australian Financial Services License.' These platforms must operate 'efficiently, honestly, and fairly,' adhering to ASIC standards concerning asset security, transaction execution, and client instruction handling.

The bill includes exemption clauses for low-risk platforms holding assets below $5,000 per client and with annual transaction volumes under $10,000,000, allowing them to bypass full licensing requirements. The government anticipates this move will unlock $24 billion in productivity growth annually, while imposing multi-million dollar fines on companies failing to protect customer assets.

data.title: Australia passes new consumer protection bill, bringing crypto platforms under financial services regulation.
data.description: Australian Treasurer Jim Chalmers and Financial Services Minister Daniel Mulino introduced the Companies Amendment (Digital Asset Framework) Bill 2025 to Parliament on Wednesday, aiming to address regulatory gaps and strengthen the protection of billions of dollars in customer assets. The bill brings crypto exchanges and custody service providers under the purview of financial services law and designates the Australian Securities and Investments Commission (ASIC) as the primary regulator. The new framework introduces two new categories of financial products: digital asset platforms and tokenized custody platforms, requiring operators to hold an “Australian Financial Services License.” These platforms must operate “efficiently, honestly, and impartially” and comply with ASIC’s custody and settlement standards regarding asset security, trade execution, and processing of customer instructions. The bill includes exemptions: low-risk platforms with less than $5,000 in assets held per customer and an annual trading volume of less than $10 million are exempt from the full licensing requirements. The government says this move is expected to unlock $24 billion in productivity growth annually, while imposing millions of dollars in fines on companies that fail to protect customer assets. (Decrypt)

data.title: Bitcoin spot ETFs saw a total net inflow of $21.1228 million yesterday, with only Fidelity FBTC experiencing a net outflow.
data.description: According to SoSoValue data, Bitcoin spot ETFs saw a total net inflow of $21.1228 million yesterday (November 26th, Eastern Time). The BlackRock ETF IBIT saw the largest single-day net inflow of $42.8249 million, bringing its historical total net inflow to $62.68 billion. Following closely were Ark Invest and 21Shares' ARKB ETF, with a net inflow of $5.9685 million, bringing its historical total net inflow to $1.74 billion. The Fidelity ETF FBTC saw the largest single-day net outflow of $33.2968 million, bringing its historical total net inflow to $11.951 billion. As of press time, the total net asset value of Bitcoin spot ETFs is $117.664 billion, the ETF net asset ratio (market capitalization as a percentage of Bitcoin's total market capitalization) is 6.56%, and the historical cumulative net inflow has reached $57.634 billion.

data.title: Ethereum spot ETFs saw a total net inflow of $60.8176 million yesterday, marking the fourth consecutive day of net inflows.
data.description: According to SoSoValue data, Ethereum spot ETFs saw a total net inflow of $60.8176 million yesterday (November 26th, Eastern Time). The Ethereum spot ETF with the largest single-day net inflow was BlackRock ETF ETHA, with a net inflow of $50.2174 million, bringing its historical total net inflow to $13.079 billion. This was followed by Grayscale Ethereum Mini Trust ETF ETH, with a single-day net inflow of $6.2745 million, bringing its historical total net inflow to $1.449 billion. As of press time, the total net asset value of Ethereum spot ETFs was $18.88 billion, with an ETF net asset value ratio (market capitalization as a percentage of total Ethereum market capitalization) of 5.17%, and a historical cumulative net inflow of $12.868 billion.

data.title: Tether Ceases Operations in Uruguay Citing High Energy Costs
data.description:
Tether has laid off 30 of its 38 staff in Uruguay, halting its massive $500 million crypto mining operations, citing high electricity costs.
source: https://cryptonews.com/news/tether-ceases-operations-in-uruguay-citing-high-energy-costs/

data.title: XRP Spot ETFs See Significant Inflows on November 26
data.description:
According to Odaily, data from SoSoValue indicates that on November 26, Eastern Time, XRP spot ETFs experienced a total net inflow of $21.81 million.

The Bitwise XRP ETF recorded the highest single-day net inflow of $7.46 million, bringing its historical total net inflow to $163 million. Following this, the Canary XRP ETF saw a net inflow of $5.21 million, with its historical total net inflow reaching $335 million.

As of the time of reporting, the total net asset value of XRP spot ETFs stands at $676 million, with an XRP net asset ratio of 0.50%. The cumulative historical net inflow has reached $644 million.

data.title: Tether's Gold Purchases May Influence Bitcoin Prices, Says BitMine CEO
data.description: According to BlockBeats, BitMine Chairman and CEO Tom Lee commented on Tether's recent gold acquisitions, noting that the stablecoin issuer has become one of the largest gold buyers globally. Lee highlighted that since early 2026, Tether has been the sole factor driving up gold prices, a trend expected to continue into early 2026. He emphasized that this situation is not detrimental to Bitcoin; rather, it suggests a potential future increase in Bitcoin prices.

data.title: The US XRP spot ETF saw a net inflow of $21.81 million in a single day.
data.description: According to SoSoValue data, XRP spot ETFs saw a total net inflow of $21.81 million yesterday (November 26th, Eastern Time). The XRP spot ETF with the largest single-day net inflow was the Bitwise XRP ETF (XRP), with a net inflow of $7.46 million, bringing its historical total net inflow for XRP to $163 million. The second largest was the Canary XRP ETF (XRPC), with a net inflow of $5.21 million, bringing its historical total net inflow for XRP to $335 million. As of press time, the total net asset value of XRP spot ETFs was $676 million, with an XRP net asset ratio of 0.50%, and a historical cumulative net inflow of $644 million.

data.title: Cathie Wood Predicts Reversal of Liquidity Crunch in AI and Cryptocurrency Sectors
data.description: According to BlockBeats, ARK Invest founder Cathie Wood has expressed that the current liquidity crunch impacting the artificial intelligence and cryptocurrency sectors is expected to reverse in the coming weeks. The market appears to agree with this outlook, and Wood emphasized that artificial intelligence is not in a bubble state.

data.title: Tether to Cease EUR₮ Redemption Services by November 2025
data.description:
According to ChainCatcher, stablecoin issuer Tether announced on November 26 that it will conclude the liquidation process of its euro stablecoin, EUR₮. As the final step in this process, Tether will halt redemption services for EUR₮ across all supported blockchains starting November 27, 2025. Previously, Tether had ceased minting EUR₮ and stopped accepting new issuance requests.

Tether's CEO, Paolo Ardoino, explained that the liquidation of EUR₮ is aimed at optimizing operational and technical infrastructure, allowing the company to focus more on networks with high utility and forward-looking initiatives. In Europe, Tether's focus will shift to the Hadron by Tether platform, which is designed to facilitate asset tokenization and provide issuance, investment, and core capital market technology for institutions, fund managers, and governments.
